artifacts.content:
From: Dan Mercer <dan.mercer.pdx@gmail.com>
To: Maya Nguyen <maya.nguyen.pt@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-01-07
Subject: Lease / moving-in brain dump

Hey love,

Typing this out because my brain’s been looping the same thoughts and I’d rather you see the whole messy version than get the half‑sentence fragments I throw out when we’re brushing our teeth.

So: my lease at 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B is up mid‑August again (I moved in 8/15/22, which feels wild). I’m paying $1,725 now, which has been fine but every time I bike past another “luxury” building on Belmont with $2,200 studios, I get this little jolt of “lock something in before it gets worse.” Michael Thompson at Thompson Property Group has been low‑key as landlords go, and it’s hard to give up being able to walk to coffee and bike downtown in under 15 minutes.

At the same time, we’re basically already living together three or four nights a week, just with extra commuting. Half my stuff is at your place in Sellwood and half of your Tupperware has mysteriously migrated to my kitchen. It feels silly to keep paying for two full places when we’re syncing groceries, meal planning, and even our Sunday calls with my parents.

I keep thinking about how my dad’s early retirement and the high blood pressure stuff has made me weirdly focused on “stability.” Part of me wants to stay exactly where I am at 742; part of me wants us to be adults and actually plan something together. Also, if I’m serious about maybe going for a lead UX role at Cascade Health Systems in the next year, it’d be nice to have the home front feeling settled instead of juggling two rents.

Maybe the first step is super practical: we sit down one evening with a shared Google Sheet, list out what we each pay now (your rent + my $1,725, utilities, internet, etc.), and play with numbers. If we did move in together, would it be your place, mine, or a new spot somewhere between Buckman and Sellwood? I don’t want to assume anything or steamroll your routines.

No pressure to answer this in one go. Maybe we can talk about it this weekend after your Saturday patients and before we start that new curry recipe?

Love,
Dan

From: Dan Mercer <dan.mercer.pdx@gmail.com>
To: Emily Carter <em.carter.oregon@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-03
Subject: Quick Eugene check‑in + holiday plotting

Hey Em,

Just got back from a soggy jog along the Eastbank Esplanade and figured I’d write before I get sucked into another rabbit hole of design blogs.

How are you and the tiny tornado? I can’t believe Oliver is five already. I was looking at that photo of him in the dinosaur pajamas you sent and realized he’s almost as tall as my bike’s top tube. Has preschool calmed down at all, or is he still coming home covered in paint and mystery glitter?

Mom and Dad seemed in decent spirits on the video call last Sunday, but I could tell Dad was downplaying how tired he’s been. Has he said anything more to you about the blood pressure stuff or money? He made a joke about “living on Costco samples” that landed a little too close to home. I keep thinking about how his early retirement wasn’t exactly optional, and now every time I tweak the budget for my $1,725 rent at 742 Evergreen Terrace I hear his voice telling me to start an emergency fund.

On that note, I finally set up automatic transfers to savings and I’m trying to be a grown‑up about it. If you ever want to compare notes on budgeting as two semi‑responsible adults who still buy too many takeout coffees, I’m game.

Thanksgiving: what’s your current plan? Maya might be able to swap a shift and drive down with me to Eugene, but we’re not sure yet. If it works, we were thinking of coming down Wednesday night and heading back Saturday morning so I’m not a zombie for work on Monday. If that doesn’t pan out, I could maybe come down solo and we do a low‑key siblings + parents thing.

Also, if you need a break at any point, you and Oliver are always welcome to come stay in Portland for a weekend. He can help me water the two plants I’ve somehow kept alive, and we can walk to the park from my place. The Buckman neighborhood is very five‑year‑old‑friendly: playgrounds, donuts, and plenty of leaves to jump in.

Text me if it’s easier, but I wanted to send a longer note. Love you.

Dan

Title: Money / future brain dump
Date: 2024-10-10

- Current rent: $1,725 at 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B (since 8/15/22). Landlord: Michael Thompson, Thompson Property Group.
- Old place: 3891 NE 15th Avenue, Unit 2 (Alberta Arts). Lived there 6/1/2019–7/31/2022, rent was $1,550. Landlord: Karen Delgado. Both places: no drama, no broken leases, no evictions, just quiet rent checks.

Stuff on my mind:

1) Emergency fund
   - Target: 6 months of expenses.
   - Motivator: Dad’s high blood pressure + early retirement. Watching him and Mom juggle bills in Eugene is a “future Dan” warning sign.
   - Action: Keep auto‑transfer to savings on payday. Don’t turn it off when I want a new bike gadget.

2) Career
   - Cascade Health Systems is…overall good. Telehealth project for older patients feels meaningful, especially thinking about Mom and Dad wrestling with portals.
   - Considering pushing for a lead UX role in the next 12–18 months.
   - Need portfolio updates: telehealth flows, patient portal redesign, user interviews.

3) Living situation
   - Lease renewal next summer. Do I:
     a) Stay at 742 (pros: location, routines, known landlord).
     b) Move in with Maya (her place in Sellwood or a new spot together).
   - Rents are creeping up. Staying put might be cheapest short‑term, but two separate apartments is dumb if we’re basically co‑living.

4) Health / stress
   - Notice stress spikes around launches. Last one = late‑night screen doomscrolling, crappy sleep.
   - Keep trying: no screens after 11, jogs along Eastbank, board game Wednesdays, monthly tech volunteer Saturdays.

5) Family
   - Keep Sunday calls with parents.
   - Check in with Emily more about Oliver and how she’s actually doing, not just kid updates.

Reminder: “Future Dan” is just “Present Dan” plus procrastination. Do small boring things now.

Event: Sunday family check‑ins
Date: 2024-09-29 (recurring weekly)
Time: 6:30–8:00 p.m.
Location: 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B (living room desk)
Attendees: Dan, Mom (Linda), Dad (Robert), Emily (sometimes Oliver), Maya (sometimes)

Description:

Loose structure so it doesn’t feel like a meeting:

6:30–6:45 p.m.
- Make French press coffee or tea.
- Open laptop at the small desk by the living room window.
- Quick texts to family: “Hopping on?”

6:45–7:30 p.m. – Video call with Mom and Dad in Eugene
- Ask Dad specifically about:
  - Blood pressure readings this week.
  - Whether he’s actually walking like the doctor told him.
  - Any weird symptoms he’s downplaying.
- Ask Mom about:
  - How she’s handling the retirement adjustment.
  - Whether they’ve gone over their budget again.
- Try not to turn it into an interrogation, just gentle nudges.

7:30–8:00 p.m. – Emily + Oliver time
- If Emily’s free, switch to a separate call or add her in.
- Let Oliver show whatever Lego / drawing / chaotic art project he’s into.
- Check in with Em about:
  - Work / single‑mom burnout.
  - Whether she wants to come up to Portland for a weekend.

Notes:
- Remember: they like seeing the apartment. Maybe give them a little tour update (plants still alive, new concert poster, bike still hanging in the hallway).
- Keep an eye on how often Dad jokes about money; jokes usually = worry.
- Try to end on something light (movie recommendations, upcoming board game café night, etc.).

From: Dan Mercer <dan.mercer.pdx@gmail.com>
To: Karen Delgado <k.delgado@delgadoresidential.com>
Date: 2022-07-05
Subject: Move‑out details for 3891 NE 15th Ave, Unit 2

Hi Karen,

Hope you’re doing well and that summer in Alberta Arts hasn’t gotten too chaotic yet.

I wanted to confirm details for my upcoming move‑out from 3891 NE 15th Avenue, Unit 2. As we discussed over the phone, my last day in the unit will be July 31, 2022. I’ve really appreciated living there since June 1, 2019 — it’s been a quiet, drama‑free few years, and you’ve been great to rent from.

A few specifics / questions:

1) Final rent
   - My understanding is that the usual $1,550 is due on July 1 for the final month, with no proration since I’m leaving on the last day. Please let me know if that’s incorrect.

2) Move‑out walkthrough
   - Are you available for a walkthrough on July 30 or 31? I’m aiming to have everything cleaned by then.
   - I’ve been careful with the place (no pets, no smoking, no major damage), so I’m hoping the security deposit will be straightforward.

3) Forwarding address
   - I’ll be moving to 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B, Portland, OR 97214 in mid‑August. I’ll send a quick note once I’m fully in and can confirm the best mailing address for the deposit return.

4) Keys / parking
   - Just to confirm: I should leave the keys on the kitchen counter and text you a photo when I’m done, and there’s nothing special I need to do about the bike storage area, right?

Thanks again for being such a reasonable landlord. This was my first place in Portland on my own, and it set the bar pretty high. If you ever need a reference from a tenant who paid on time and didn’t cause trouble, I’m happy to vouch for you.

Best,

Dan Mercer
(503) 734‑9826
3891 NE 15th Ave, Unit 2

metadata.subject: Move‑out details for 3891 NE 15th Ave, Unit 2

artifacts.content:
From: Dan Mercer <dan.mercer.pdx@gmail.com>
To: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsonpropertygroup.com>
Date: 2022-08-02
Subject: Move‑in logistics for 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B

Hi Michael,

I’m looking forward to moving into 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B later this month and wanted to confirm a few details so moving day goes smoothly.

Here’s what I have so far:

- Move‑in date: Monday, August 15, 2022
- Monthly rent: $1,725
- Property management address I have on file: Thompson Property Group, 1200 SE Morrison St, Suite 450, Portland, OR 97214
- Phone numbers: (503) 555‑1934 and alternate (503) 555‑7462

Questions / confirmations:

1) Payment
   - I’ll bring the cashier’s check for first month’s rent + security deposit on 8/15 unless you’d prefer I drop it at the SE Morrison office earlier.
   - Going forward, is online payment through your portal preferred, or do you still accept checks mailed to the office address above?

2) Access on move‑in day
   - What’s the best way to get the keys? Will someone meet me at the building, or should I pick them up at your office earlier that morning?
   - Is there a loading zone I can use on Evergreen Terrace for a couple of hours while we move furniture in?

3) Utilities
   - I’ve already set up PGE and internet to start on 8/15. Anything else in the building (e.g., shared garbage/recycling details, bike storage) I should know about ahead of time?

4) Contact
   - For routine stuff (maintenance requests, questions), should I use your main office line at (503) 555‑1934, the alternate number, or email?

I’m excited to be closer to the inner eastside — I bike to work near the Convention Center, so being able to ride over the Hawthorne instead of coming in from Alberta will be a big upgrade.

Thanks again for your help getting everything set up.

Best,

Dan Mercer
Future tenant – 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B
Phone: (503) 734‑9826

metadata.subject: Move‑in logistics for 742 Evergreen Terrace, Apt 3B

artifacts.content:
Title: Weekday routine – what’s actually working
Date: 2024-06-18

Morning
- 7:00 a.m.: Alarm, French press coffee.
- 7:15–7:45: Scroll design blogs / tech news (limit this; easy to lose 45 minutes).
- Office days (2–3x/week): Bike from 742 Evergreen Terrace across Hawthorne Bridge to Cascade Health Systems office near the Convention Center.
- WFH days: Log into Teams from the living room desk by the window.

Work
- Current focus: simplifying telehealth appointment flows for older patients.
- Motivation: Mom and Dad struggling with online portals; want Cascade’s stuff to be less confusing.
- Try to keep blocks for deep work (wireframes, user flows) 9:30–11:30 before meetings eat the day.

Afternoons
- Quick walk around Buckman or down to the river between 3:30–4:00 when possible.
- If not raining, jog along Eastbank Esplanade 2–3x/week.

Evenings
- Simple dinners: tofu stir‑fry, roasted veggies, sheet‑pan anything.
- Maya over ~3 nights/week, I go to her place in Sellwood ~1 night.
- Wednesdays: Board game café on SE Division with friends.
- 1 Saturday/month: Volunteer at community tech workshop (older adults + smartphones/laptops).

Habits to reinforce
- No screens after 11:00 p.m. (sleep is way better when I stick to this).
- Keep Sunday for:
  - Family video calls.
  - Meal prep / grocery planning.
  - Light budgeting check‑in (remember Dad’s situation = warning sign).

Longer‑term
- Keep notes on projects that could support a future lead UX role.
- Start a tiny freelance side gig only if it doesn’t wreck the above routine.

Hey Emily,

Hope you’re doing well! I wanted to catch you up on a few things happening on my end and see how you and Oliver are doing. It’s been a busy few weeks here, but good busy.

Work at Cascade Health Systems has been intense but rewarding. We’ve been focusing on making telehealth more user-friendly for seniors, and it’s really hitting home for me, thinking about Mom and Dad trying to navigate all that tech. I’ve been reading up on ways to simplify things, and it’s been enlightening.

Speaking of family, I had a nice long chat with Mom and Dad last Sunday. Dad’s blood pressure is still a concern, but he’s taking it in stride. I’ve been thinking a lot about their future, especially Dad’s early retirement. It’s pushed me to start a proper emergency fund and consider some budgeting strategies. 

On a lighter note, Maya and I have been talking about possibly moving in together next year when my lease is up. I love my current spot at 742 Evergreen Terrace, but the rent is steep, and I’ve been seeing prices go up all around Portland. I’ve been reminiscing about my last place at the Alberta Arts area. Do you remember that? I was paying $1,550 there, and my landlord, Karen Delgado, was always easy to reach at Delgado Residential on 2105 NE Alberta St. I still have her numbers saved—(503) 555-8821 and (503) 555-1174. It’s all making me think about the stability I have now versus the rising costs.

Maya and I are also considering taking a trip—maybe to Vancouver or down the California coast—before things get too serious with family and career decisions. It’d be nice to have a break and see some new places together.

Anyway, I’d love to hear how things are on your end. How’s Oliver doing? Is he still obsessed with dinosaurs? Let’s plan a get-together soon; maybe I can come over and we can cook something up while Oliver shows me his latest dino collection.

Take care and give my love to Oliver!

Dan

metadata.subject: Catching Up and Thoughts on the Future
